what_it_does: This file is a ledger entry documenting the downloaded but not-yet-served GGUF model Qwen3.6-35B-A3B IQ3_S variant, including its MoE architecture, IQ3_S quantization, disk size, source, intended use as a lower-bit fallback, Ollama launch configuration, quirks, open questions, and links to related entries.
observations: Frontmatter marks type=model, status=downloaded, runtime=not-yet-served, date_added=2026-05-27, and tags include foundation/models/chat/gguf/moe.
The model is a 35B total / ~3B active per token MoE quantized to GGUF IQ3_S (importance-weighted 3-bit) by unsloth; the file is 12.7 GB and the disk snapshot is 13 GB.
It was pulled alongside the Q3_K_M sibling as a lower-bit fallback to gain roughly 3 GB of VRAM headroom at the cost of slightly more quantization error.
It is not registered with Ollama and has not been served; the launch config repeats the ollama create pattern used for the Q3_K_M variant.
The Open at this entry section explicitly asks for a head-to-head quality comparison, the actual VRAM headroom value, and whether to keep both variants long-term.
standouts: It documents a not-yet-served fallback quant variant rather than a currently running model.
The central framing is a quality-versus-VRAM-headroom tradeoff against the Q3_K_M sibling.
The entry ends with unresolved keep/drop/evaluation decisions rather than conclusions.
conventions: YAML frontmatter with id, filename, status, runtime state, date_added, and tags.
Wikilink-style internal references such as [[qwen3_6-35b-a3b-gguf-q3-k-m]] and [[../operations/ollama]].
At-a-glance table followed by Source, Fits these needs, Tested performance, Known-good launch config, Quirks, Open at this entry, and Connects to sections.
Uses a code block for the Ollama Modelfile command.
concerns: Status says downloaded but no runtime data exists; performance claims are speculative.
Quality comparison with the Q3_K_M sibling is asserted but not measured in this entry.
date_added (2026-05-27) is later than today if interpreted literally, which may indicate a planned/future pull or metadata error.
Open decisions are unresolved, so the file functions as a decision stub rather than a finalized record.
notes: A ledger stub for a lower-bit MoE fallback that is downloaded but still needs Ollama registration, quality testing, and a keep/drop decision.
questions: What is the measured quality gap versus the Q3_K_M sibling on the operator's typical queries?
How much VRAM does this variant actually consume at the target context length once Ollama's KV cache is included?
Which deployment scenario does the extra ~3 GB of headroom enable (larger context, co-resident service, or something else)?
Will Ollama registration succeed with the same Modelfile pattern, and does per-token latency differ meaningfully from K-quants?
Is the IQ3_S variant worth keeping on disk once the Q3_K_M variant is validated?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record a candidate lower-bit model variant and its tradeoffs; the file does exactly that, though it contains no actual test results.
apparent_themes: Quantization quality versus memory headroom tradeoffs
Deployment planning for Ollama-served MoE models
Experimental fallback options and decision stubs
intention_signals: The entry appears to be trying to preserve the rationale for pulling a lower-bit quant variant so the operator can later decide whether the VRAM savings justify any quality loss relative to the higher-bit sibling.
novelty: It treats a downloaded model file as a decision node, pairing it with a sibling variant and explicitly framing the unresolved keep/drop/evaluation questions.
contribution: It contributes a structured record of the IQ3_S variant's existence, specs, source, launch pattern, tradeoffs, and open evaluation questions, which a fused system would keep to support deployment planning and the eventual keep-or-drop decision.
summary_for_embedding: Ledger entry for the downloaded but not-yet-served Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-GGUF IQ3_S MoE chat model, describing its 3-bit importance-weighted quantization, 13 GB disk footprint, lower-bit fallback role, Ollama registration steps, and unresolved quality/VRAM tradeoff questions.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to track a candidate lower-bit fallback variant and guide a future decision between it and the higher-bit Q3_K_M sibling based on runtime headroom and output quality.
